old_claim.note: Basic Woodworking is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No woodworking dependency remains because the scoped Vindolanda evidence consists of leather panels and does not preserve or analyze a tent frame or poles.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract reports species analysis of eight leather panels from the same tent and does not include wooden frame components in the analyzed artifact.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The direct evidence and node scope are the cut hide panels; no pole, frame, or woodworking trace is part of the analyzed set.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Likely use of poles in an assembled tent does not justify importing a frame dependency into a node explicitly narrowed to surviving panels.

ontology_before: The old node combined hypothetical hide covering and wooden frame into one unsupported prehistoric claim.
ontology_after: The revised node represents only the surviving Roman leather-panel evidence.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: hide_tent_frames no longer directly depends on woodworking_basic.
would_reject_if: The node were respecified to include a directly evidenced and analyzed wooden tent frame.
short_reason: No wooden frame is part of the scoped archaeological evidence.
